title: Biological Neurons
description: Quick check on the biological inspiration for artificial neural networks.

questions.prompt: Name the 4 parts of a biological neuron mentioned in this module.
What is found on the surface of a dendrite to receive signals from other neurons (one per synapse)?
What is at the end of an axon that connects with the dendrites of other neurons?
Match each structure of a neuron to its role.
right: Transmits an action potential from the soma to other neurons
Body of the neuron: where the nucleus, DNA, and protein synthesis live
The point at which two neurons meet
Sums incoming potentials and fires an action potential when the threshold is exceeded
Receive action potentials from other neurons

questions.prompt: Where are action potentials summed and measured against a threshold?
Name the three types of neuron.
The CNS is only the brain and the spinal cord. Sensory neurons elsewhere in the body (in sense organs like the eye, ear, nose, and tongue) are part of which nervous system?
